items.name: breath-cycle
items.description: Inhale → hold → exhale. Auto-breathe at 50%/70%/85%. Commands: `/exhale`, `/breathe`, `/rest`, `/inhale`. Write preload with resume point + orient targets before rotating.
items.author: Curtis Mercier

items.name: community-safe
items.description: > How Soma keeps private data out of public content. The channel-guard script catches leaks mechanically — this protocol covers the judgment that prevents creating them.
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: correction-capture
items.description: When corrected, the old behavior was wrong. Don't explain why you did it — learn why it was wrong. Log `old → new`. First time: adjust behavior. Second time: write a muscle so it sticks. Third time: it becomes protocol or identity — something that shapes every session, not just this one. The "use your tools" correction happened 4+ times before it stuck. More text didn't fix it. What fixed it was making the tools fit the task. When a correction keeps recurring, the fix isn't discipline — it's architecture. Own mistakes with honesty, not submission.
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: detection-triggers
items.description: Capture on: corrections, preferences, 3+ repeated patterns, knowledge gaps. Skip one-time instructions. Bubble-up: session log → muscle → protocol → core.
items.author: Curtis Mercier

items.name: frontmatter-standard
items.description: Every `.md` file needs YAML frontmatter: `type`, `status`, `created`, `updated`. Protocols get `## TL;DR`; muscles get `<!-- digest:start/end -->`.
items.author: Curtis Mercier

items.name: heat-tracking
items.description: Cold (0-2): skipped. Warm (3-7): TL;DR only. Hot (8+): full body. Heat decays each session, bumps on use. `/pin` heats, `/kill` drops to 0.
items.author: Curtis Mercier

items.name: maps
items.description: MAPS — My Automation Protocol Scripts. Before any task, check for a MAP. After any repeated task, build one. MAPS connect AMPS into repeatable processes. Refine on every use.
items.author: Curtis Mercier
items.breadcrumb: MAPS — My Automation Protocol Scripts. Before any task, check for a MAP. After any repeated task, build one. MAPS connect AMPS into repeatable processes. Refine on every use.

items.name: pattern-evolution
items.description: Maturation: Skills → Muscles → Protocols → Automations. Born from gaps and repeated friction. Not every pattern climbs the full ladder.
items.author: Curtis Mercier

items.name: plan-hygiene
items.description: Plans rot. Frontmatter needs status, remaining, tooling. Empty remaining = complete → archive. Budget: ≤12 active. Verify on exhale.
items.author: Curtis Mercier
items.breadcrumb: Plans rot. Frontmatter needs status, remaining, tooling. Empty remaining = complete → archive. Budget: ≤12 active. Verify on exhale.

items.name: pre-flight
items.description: Before building anything new, check if it exists. Grep codebase, scan `.soma/`, verify need. Catch duplication before executing.
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: quality-standards
items.description: Never delete — move or archive. Clean atomic commits with descriptive messages. Push when ready. Know which branch deploys. Confirm before touching critical files. Close the loop — when fixing a bug, fix the system that allowed it. Tests match shipped code, not planned features. Use conventional commit format: `type(scope): description`.
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: response-style
items.description: Prefer prose over bullets. Minimum formatting for the content. One question per response max. No hollow social scripts.
items.author: meetsoma
items.breadcrumb: Prefer prose over bullets. Minimum formatting for the content. One question per response max. No hollow social scripts.

items.name: session-checkpoints
items.description: Two git tracks: `.soma/` auto-commits on exhale (diff-on-boot), project code gets checkpoint commits (squash before push). Settings: `checkpoints.*`.
items.author: Curtis Mercier

items.name: task-tracking
items.description: One board: `.soma/_kanban.md`. Four lanes: Active, In Progress, Done, Parked. Move cards in real time. Verify board on exhale.
items.author: Curtis Mercier

items.name: tool-discipline
items.description: Scripts first, raw commands second. Read before edit. Build tools for yourself — when you do the same thing twice manually, make a script. Guard auto-blocks dangerous bash. The agent that builds its own tools gets faster every session.
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: workflow
items.description: **Test before commit.** Run the relevant test suite before staging. Don't commit code you haven't verified. **Know your deploy branch.** Identify which branch triggers deployment (`main`, `production`, `release/*`) before your first push. Ask if unsure. **Never leave unpushed commits.** Unpushed work is invisible to teammates, CI, and backups. If you're done working, push. If you're not ready to push, you're not done.
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: working-style
items.description: Test → commit → push → log. No unpushed, untested, or unlogged work. `soma-code.sh map` before editing any file. `soma-refactor.sh scan` before deleting or renaming anything. `soma-verify.sh` after structural changes. `soma-ship.sh` after every commit to agent. Plans live in files, not in context — write it down, link it, move on. When you catch a bug, fix the system that allowed it. When a test fails, trace BOTH the test and the code — don't change the test to make it pass without understanding why it failed. Be direct. Lead with action. No ceremony.
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: doc-hygiene
items.description: <!-- digest:start --> > **Doc Hygiene** — plans rot. Update `remaining` on every touch. Empty → archive. After shipping, scan for stale docs. Verify docs match code before referencing. <!-- digest:end
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: icon-audit
items.description: <!-- digest:start --> > **Icon Audit** — browse icon libraries, extract SVGs, build numbered comparison pages, screenshot for visual AI review. Works with any SVG library (Lucide, Phosphor, etc). <!--
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: incremental-refactor
items.description: <!-- digest:start --> > **Incremental Refactor** — never refactor blind. Phase: (1) scan → `soma-refactor.sh scan/refs/graph/tags` to map dependencies + references, (2) route audit → `soma-refactor.sh
items.author: Unknown

items.name: micro-exhale
items.description: <!-- digest:start --> > **Micro-Exhale** — after major completions, append `## HH:MM` summary to session log. One file per day, read first, never overwrite. Checkpoint within session, not a full exhal
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: precision-edit
items.description: <!-- digest:start --> > **Precision Edit** — before editing any multi-line file, extract content with line numbers first. (1) Run relevant verify script (`soma-verify.sh copy`, `soma-verify-styles.sh`
items.author: Unknown

items.name: safe-file-ops
items.description: ## Digest <!-- digest:start --> Four rules: (1) Never `write` without checking if the file exists first — write overwrites silently. Use `ls` then `read` then decide: `edit`, append, or write new. (2)
items.author: Unknown

items.name: task-tooling
items.description: <!-- digest:start --> > **Before touching code, name your tools.** For each phase of the task, which script handles it? Which muscle applies? Where's the MAP? When there's no tool for a phase, say so
items.author: Unknown

items.name: test-hygiene
items.description: <!-- digest:start --> Validate tests aren't stale after any code removal, rename, or restructure. Run all suites, grep for orphaned references, check pass counts. Dead tests are worse than no tests —
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: debug
items.description: Systematic approach to finding and fixing bugs. Optimized to avoid common agent traps: guessing, naive text analysis, and chasing false leads.
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: refactor
items.description: Extract, move, or restructure code safely. Follows an incremental pattern — scan first, plan the blast radius, execute one concern at a time, verify after every change.
items.author: meetsoma

items.name: visual-gap-analysis
items.description: Map any workflow end-to-end as an ASCII diagram. Identify every handoff, every external dependency, every place where things can fail. Then TEST it — diagrams find bugs, E2E tests prove fixes.
items.author: meetsoma
